title: mildly-spicy wood-fired thai chicken bake
description: a quickly prepared thai-inspired dish featuring chicken cooked in green salsa and baked in a wood-fired oven. kosher-friendly and easy to make.
instructions: preheat the oven to 425 degrees fahrenheit.
mix together coconut oil, grated ginger, garlic powder, kosher salt, and black pepper in a small bowl.
place the chicken breasts on a hot stone plate and brush them with the spice mixture.
spread the green salsa evenly over the top of each chicken breast.
place the hot stone plate in the oven and bake for 12-15 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.
remove the chicken from the oven and garnish with fresh cilantro.
serve the chicken hot and enjoy!
meanwhile, heat a small non-stick pan over medium heat.
add a small amount of coconut oil to the pan and sauté the chicken for an additional 2-3 minutes on each side.
serve the chicken with additional green salsa on the side, if desired.
